title: Clawster Electron to Tauri v2 Rewrite
description: Full 1:1 port of Clawster desktop pet app from Electron to Tauri v2. Rust backend replaces Electron main process; React frontend is preserved with IPC bridge adapted to Tauri invoke/listen.

stories.title: Tauri v2 project scaffolding
stories.description: Initialize Tauri v2 project structure alongside existing React frontend. Configure Cargo.toml, tauri.conf.json, capabilities, and Vite integration.
acceptanceCriteria: src-tauri/ directory exists with Cargo.toml, tauri.conf.json, src/main.rs, src/lib.rs
Cargo.toml includes tauri v2, serde, serde_json, tokio dependencies
tauri.conf.json configures app identifier 'com.clawster.app', main window, and build commands
npm run tauri dev launches the app with the React frontend (vite dev server) without errors
A basic transparent, frameless window appears on screen

stories.title: Persistent config store (Rust)
stories.description: Implement Rust equivalent of electron-store using tauri-plugin-store or custom JSON file store. Mirror the full StoreSchema from store.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: src-tauri/src/store.rs exists with StoreSchema struct matching all fields from Electron store.ts
Default values match the Electron defaults exactly (clawbot url, watch settings, pet settings, hotkeys, onboarding, tutorial, dev)
Store reads/writes persist to disk across app restarts
get_settings and update_settings Tauri commands are registered and callable from frontend

stories.title: Multi-window management
stories.description: Create all 8 windows matching Electron: pet, pet-chat, assistant, chatbar, screenshot-question, onboarding, pet-context-menu, workspace-browser. Each window must use its corresponding HTML entry point.
acceptanceCriteria: Pet window: transparent, frameless, always-on-top, 164x164, click-through when not interacting
All 8 window creation functions exist in src-tauri/src/windows.rs
Each window loads its correct HTML entry point (e.g., pet.html, assistant.html, chatbar.html)
Window positioning logic works: pet-chat anchors above pet, assistant anchors above pet, workspace-browser anchors above pet
Windows can be shown/hidden via Tauri commands

stories.title: Frontend IPC bridge (Tauri invoke/listen)
stories.description: Replace preload.ts window.clawster API with a Tauri-compatible bridge. All ~80 IPC methods must be ported to use @tauri-apps/api invoke() and listen().
acceptanceCriteria: src/renderer/tauri-bridge.ts exists exporting a ClawsterAPI-compatible object
All invoke-based methods (getSettings, updateSettings, captureScreen, sendToClawbot, etc.) call Tauri invoke()
All event listeners (onClawbotMood, onActivityEvent, onPetMoving, etc.) use Tauri listen()
All send-based methods (dragPet, petClicked, toggleAssistant, etc.) call Tauri invoke() or emit()
window.clawster is populated from the bridge (no preload.ts needed)
TypeScript compiles with no errors

stories.title: ClawBot HTTP client (Rust)
stories.description: Port ClawBotClient from TypeScript to Rust using reqwest. Support Responses API, Chat Completions fallback, SSE streaming, connection health checks, suggestion polling, event sending, and cron polling.
acceptanceCriteria: src-tauri/src/clawbot.rs exists with ClawBotClient struct
Health check, suggestion polling, and event sending work via reqwest
chat() method supports Responses API with Chat Completions fallback
chatStream() method supports SSE streaming via both APIs
Action parsing (parseActionFromResponse) correctly extracts action JSON blocks from response text
Cron job polling works and emits events to frontend
Connection status changes emit events to all windows

stories.title: File and app watchers (Rust)
stories.description: Port Watchers class to Rust. File watching via notify crate, active app detection via macOS accessibility APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: src-tauri/src/watchers.rs exists with file watcher using notify crate
File watcher emits file_added, file_changed, file_deleted events matching Electron format
Active app watcher detects frontmost app name on macOS
Window title detection works via osascript JXA (matching window-title.ts logic)
Watchers respect store settings (watch.activeApp, watch.sendWindowTitles, watch.folders)
Activity events are forwarded to ClawBot and emitted to frontend windows

stories.title: Pet behaviors (Rust)
stories.description: Port all pet behavior systems: attention seeker, idle behaviors (blink, look_around, snip_claws, yawn, stretch, wiggle, wander), sleep/wake cycle, pet movement animation, and pet action execution.
acceptanceCriteria: animateMoveTo smoothly moves pet window at ~60fps with ease-out curve
Attention seeker periodically moves pet toward cursor when distance > 600px
All 7 idle behaviors fire with correct weighted randomization
Sleep system: pet dozes after 60s idle, fully sleeps after 5s more, wakes on interaction with startle animation
Pet actions (set_mood, move_to, move_to_cursor, snip, wave, look_at) all work from ClawBot responses
Pet dragging updates position and anchored windows follow

stories.title: Screen capture (Rust)
stories.description: Port screen capture: native macOS screencapture command, permission checking, camera snap animation coordination, and captureScreenWithContext.
acceptanceCriteria: captureScreen Tauri command captures screen on macOS via screencapture CLI
Permission status check works (granted/denied/not-determined/restricted)
Camera snap animation is triggered before capture with correct timing delays
captureScreenWithContext returns image + cursor position + screen size
Screenshot question flow works end-to-end (capture → show question window → send to ClawBot)

stories.title: Global hotkeys and system tray
stories.description: Port global shortcuts (openChat, captureScreen, openAssistant) and system tray with menu items.
acceptanceCriteria: Global hotkeys register on app start using tauri-plugin-global-shortcut
Default hotkeys match Electron: Cmd+Shift+Space (chat), Cmd+Shift+/ (capture), Cmd+Shift+A (assistant)
Hotkeys can be updated at runtime when settings change
System tray shows with correct icon and menu (Open Assistant, Open Chat, Settings, Quit)
Tray menu actions work correctly

stories.title: Onboarding and tutorial system
stories.description: Port onboarding flow (workspace setup, gateway validation, personality config) and tutorial manager (10-step interactive tutorial).
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding window shows on first launch when onboarding.completed is false
Gateway validation (validateGateway command) tests connection to OpenClaw
Workspace creation (createClawsterWorkspace) creates directory structure with identity/soul files
OpenClaw workspace detection reads ~/.openclaw config and workspace
Tutorial manager tracks 10 steps with correct progression logic
Tutorial cursor tracking and follow behavior work for step 4
Tutorial skip/resume/replay all work correctly

stories.title: Workspace browser and file preview
stories.description: Port workspace file browser: directory listing, file opening, path revealing, and file preview (markdown, JSON, images including HEIC transcoding).
acceptanceCriteria: listWorkspaceDirectory returns entries with name, path, kind, timestamps
openWorkspacePath opens files/dirs in system default app
revealWorkspacePath shows item in Finder
previewWorkspaceFile supports markdown, JSON (pretty-printed), and image previews
HEIC images are transcoded to PNG via sharp or sips fallback
Path traversal is prevented (outside_workspace error for ../ paths)
File size limits are enforced (2MB markdown/JSON, 12MB images)

stories.title: Auto-updater and build configuration
stories.description: Configure tauri-plugin-updater for auto-updates, macOS code signing and notarization, and build targets matching Electron (DMG, ZIP for Mac).
acceptanceCriteria: tauri.conf.json has updater config pointing to clawster.pet/downloads
macOS signing identity configured (Yuwen Wu 39428U49B4)
npm run tauri build produces a working DMG and ZIP for macOS arm64
Auto-updater checks for updates on app start
App version matches package.json version

stories.title: Tutorial system (full implementation)
stories.description: Replace tutorial stub commands with full TutorialManager implementation matching Electron's 10-step interactive tutorial with cursor tracking, follow behavior, step progression, and events.
acceptanceCriteria: TutorialManager struct in src-tauri/src/tutorial.rs with 10-step progression
Tutorial commands (tutorial_pet_clicked, tutorial_next, tutorial_skip, tutorial_resume, tutorial_start_over, tutorial_open_panel) all have real implementations
Tutorial emits tutorial-step, tutorial-hint, tutorial-ended, tutorial-resume-prompt events
Step 4 cursor tracking and follow behavior work
Pet window expand/contract for tutorial speech bubble works
replay_tutorial resets state and starts from step 1
get_tutorial_status returns real isActive/currentStep/completed values

stories.title: Idle detection and chat popup suggestions
stories.description: Port idle detection system (5-min threshold) and proactive chat popup suggestions triggered by idle state and app switches.
acceptanceCriteria: Idle detection timer fires after 5 minutes of no activity
sendChatPopup function sends idle/app_switch/proactive triggers to ClawBot
Chat popup events emitted to pet window with id, text, trigger, quickReplies
App switch chat cooldown (60s) prevents spam
Idle timer resets on user activity (pet click, chat, etc.)

stories.title: App launch on startup
stories.description: Implement setLaunchOnStartup for macOS (login items), respecting the onboarding launchOnStartup setting.
acceptanceCriteria: set_launch_on_startup Tauri command exists
On macOS: creates/removes login item via osascript or launchd plist
Onboarding complete handler calls set_launch_on_startup with user's choice
Setting persists across app restarts

stories.title: Runtime panic fix - deferred async initialization
stories.description: Fix the 'no reactor running' panic that occurs when tokio::spawn/tauri::async_runtime::spawn is called during setup() before the Tauri runtime is ready.
acceptanceCriteria: App starts without panic on 'npm run tauri:dev'
Pet window appears on screen
Background polling (ClawBot connection check) starts after app launch
No 'there is no reactor running' error in console
